title: New demos directory with a new build from scratch demo script and a d…
body:
…emos dir README.md file

A new demos dir. 
A README.md file for that dir.
A buildah-scratch-demo.sh

Signed-off-by: William Henry @ipbabble <whenry@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Nice 😄 

Minor typo for word 'demo' in comments:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/668/commits/5cd2242d7b8a5df0e7394751e868713d0cc80a39#diff-5d1d1ffdb48db1dc7f6af352100a128aR15


Could this message give some props to buildah 🔨?
> This is a new cloud native container built using buildah

https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/668/files#diff-5d1d1ffdb48db1dc7f6af352100a128aR51

The `quayuser` variable here is using braces outside of quotes (which I don't have an issue with, just isn't consistent with others in the script).
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/668/files#diff-5d1d1ffdb48db1dc7f6af352100a128aR75

This demo also assumes Fedora (dnf), should it also support yum for EL7? Happy to raise a PR to add el7 support after the commit if we want to do it that way (I generally pitch to enterprise EL7 customers, understand everyone else is over on Fedora!).

Thanks @pixdrift 
I'll fix it up for Yum at a later time. I'm not sure if it's just a matter of just having a:

    # Change dnf to yum here if you are using RHEL 
    pkgmgr=dnf  
    ...
    $pkgmgr -y install .....etc.

Or if the syntax would change .. e.g. is installroot the same? I can't remember but I think there was a change.

In that case it requires maybe some if statements where debian users can insert their code too.
dmeo typo, props, quayuser usage fixed.
@pixdrift also you'll notice that I don't use the entrypoint or cmd in the example. I explicitly call out the command I want to run. Also I test the entrypoint by using Podman later in the script.

@ipbabble, yep, noticed and appreciated that subtle point (run/entrypoint with podman) 😉👍 

I will run this script through el7 and see how it goes. I had issues with podman + rpm deps last time I was playing with podman on el7, so will check that out too.
@ipbabble gotta shorten up the commit message.  Can't be over 90 chars.

I won't get to these fixes until this evening. 
Thanks for all the feedback :)
I think I need to repush this. Wait until I check.
Yes. Wait. I need to push the correct script. My mistake.

I think this is a build system issue - the registry. Can someone restart?

I was going to get this one merged before I start the Docker compatibility demo on a separate branch. fair enough?
I'm going to try to merge this.  One small tweak to the README.md for the next round.  Build /Test issues are not related.

:pushpin: Commit 8893f47 has been approved by `TomSweeneyRedHat`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=TomSweeneyRedHat 8893f478de72bacf778f5a6d6a23246384c13a30 -->

Approved by: TomSweeneyRedHat
Pushing 2ea3e11d18667dcf480285d61ccf98e2d01569f7 to master...
I think at the very least that requires a medal emoticon 🥇 nice one @ipbabble!
